title: You want to make it harder for someone to clone your proprietary churn-prediction model just by querying its API. What defenses would you actually deploy, and which ones are mostly theater?
prompt:
You want to make it harder for someone to clone your proprietary churn-prediction model just by querying its API. What defenses would you actually deploy, and which ones are mostly theater?

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
Real defenses: rate limiting and query budgets tuned by cost and diversity, not just raw count; output coarsening — a risk tier instead of a precise probability, since precise numbers make distillation training efficient; ongoing monitoring for extraction query patterns; and contractual controls like API terms of service, which matter if you ever need to take action. Mostly theater: a one-time "we added noise" fix with no ongoing monitoring, since a patient attacker with enough queries can statistically average noise out, and watermarking alone if you never actually check for it.

**Takeaway:** If your model is exposed as a queryable API at all, you cannot make extraction impossible, only more expensive and more detectable — combine technical controls with legal ones.
hint_notes: Real: diversity-aware rate limits, output coarsening (risk tier not raw probability), ongoing extraction-pattern monitoring, contractual ToS. Theater: one-time noise injection (averages out over many queries), watermarking nobody checks.
